title: Fermented Gooey Adventurous Chinese Apple Stir-fry
description: This vegan-friendly stir-fry combines the unique flavors of fermented foods with sweet and tangy apples, creating an adventurous yet comforting meal.

ingredients.name: 1 small apple
ingredients.notes: preferably a variety with high sugar content like Fuji or Honeycrisp

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup fermented black beans
ingredients.notes: ensure they are thoroughly washed and soaked in water for at least 30 minutes

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon soy sauce (tamari)
ingredients.notes: adjust to taste, as tamari is saltier than traditional soy sauce

ingredients.name: Juice of 1 lime (optional)
ingredients.notes: to balance the sweet and savory flavors

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: ensure it is vegan-friendly if using animal-derived products

ingredients.name: 1 cup shredded daikon radish (optional)
ingredients.notes: can add crunch and umami flavor to the dish

instructions: Wash all ingredients thoroughly. If using lime, ensure it is freshly squeezed.
Chop apples into bite-sized pieces. Soak fermented black beans in water for at least 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and daikon (if using), stirring for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the apple pieces to the pan, cooking for another 2-3 minutes until they start to soften slightly.
Drain the fermented black beans, add them to the wok, along with any soaking water if available. Stir-fry for an additional 1 minute.
Pour in soy sauce (or tamari), and lime juice (if using). Mix well and continue to stir-fry until the apple pieces are heated through and slightly caramelized.
Remove from heat and serve hot over steamed rice or noodles.
